methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.0B - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Credo Semiconductor
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $5B+

private.name: Ouster
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $1.5B

private.name: Aeva Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Astera Labs operates in the Chips & Semiconductors sector as a publicly traded company. The peer universe comprises 3 public comparables (Credo Semiconductor, Ouster, Aeva Technologies) and 8 private peers, providing a reasonably sized reference set for same-sector, same-stage valuation anchoring.

The peer-median method is the primary applicable triangulation approach. Across the 3 public semiconductor peers, valuations range from $1.0B (Aeva) to $5.0B (Credo), with a median of $1.5B. This dispersion reflects heterogeneity in scale and market positioning within the sector. The peer-median method yields an implied valuation of $1.5B. A revenue-based public-comp anchoring approach cannot be applied because the target's revenue is not on file, limiting reliance on price-to-sales multiples despite a sector median of 24.4x across ALAB, NVDA, and AMD.

The triangulated valuation range is $1.3B to $1.7B, centered on a median of $1.5B. This range reflects the peer-median anchor with conservative downside and upside adjustments. Key uncertainties include Astera Labs' revenue scale and growth trajectory relative to the peer set, as well as product differentiation and market share dynamics within the semiconductor infrastructure market. The absence of multiple independent methods constrains confidence; revenue disclosure would enable P/S triangulation and tighten the range materially.
